scenes.voice: Hey there, young explorers! Today we're going on an adventure to learn about forces and motion. Are you ready to discover what makes things move?
A force is a push or a pull that can move an object. Imagine you're playing tug-of-war with your friends. You're using force to pull the rope.
Gravity is a force that pulls us towards the Earth. That's why you don't float off into space when you're standing on the ground.
Friction is a force that opposes motion. Imagine you're sliding on a slippery floor. The friction is making it hard for you to move.
Speed and direction are two things that can affect motion. If you're moving fast, you'll cover more ground. And if you're moving in a straight line, you'll get to your destination quickly.
What is gravity? (Answer: Gravity is a force that pulls us towards the Earth.)

scenes.subtext: You did it! Review what you've learned.
scenes.voice: Today we learned about forces and motion. We discovered that forces can be pushes or pulls, and that gravity is a force that pulls us towards the Earth. And we also learned about friction and how it can affect motion. Great job, young explorers! You're now experts on forces and motion!
